articles.title: RSS Is Back From the Dead -- and It Might Be the Only Sane Way to Read News in 2026
articles.summary: While AI aggregators race to score and rank your reality, web-based RSS readers are quietly offering the thing no algorithm can: a feed you actually chose. FeedBucket's 2026 roundup cuts through the noise with five readers built for people who want signal without surveillance. The real insight: the best news product in 2026 is defined by what it leaves out.

articles.title: The Solo Creator With a $150/Month AI Stack Is Outpacing Your Entire Production Team
articles.summary: ProductionHub maps the real industry shift: AI isn't replacing crews overnight, but solo operators wiring together Make, Runway, and ElevenLabs are publishing 20 videos a day while studios are still in their kickoff call. The algorithm can't tell the difference.

articles.title: A Therapist Reviewed Sunsama for ADHD -- The Overcommitment Warning Is the Whole Point
articles.summary: A Saskatchewan ADHD therapist breaks down why Sunsama's gentle nudge when you plan past 5-6 hours of focused work is more therapeutically valuable than any productivity hack. Most ADHD brains plan for 10+ hours; the research says 3-4 is realistic.

articles.title: Project Censored Turns 50 -- Here Are the Stories Corporate Media Buried This Year
articles.summary: The 2026 list leads with ICE soliciting social media surveillance contracts to identify critics, then threads through tech-enabled union busting at Amazon, privatized homeless camp sweeps, and the near-total absence of working-class Americans in state legislatures.
